sentences.text: The A-extension offers atomic memory operation (AMO) instructions for words, doublewords, and quadwords (only for `AMOCAS`).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The absence of atomic operations for subword data types necessitates emulation strategies.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For bitwise operations, this emulation can be performed via word-sized bitwise AMO* instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For non-bitwise operations, emulation is achievable using word-sized `LR`/`SC` instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Several limitations arise from this emulation approach:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: In systems with large-scale or Non-Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) configurations, emulation based on `LR`/`SC` introduces issues related to scalability and fairness, particularly under conditions of high contention.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Emulation of narrower AMOs through wider AMO* instructions on non-idempotent IO memory regions may result in unintended side effects.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Utilizing wider AMO* instructions for emulating narrower AMOs risks activating extraneous breakpoints or watchpoints.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: In the absence of native support for subword atomics, compilers often resort to inlining code sequences to provide the required emulation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: This practice contributes to an increase in code size, with consequent impacts on system performance and memory utilization.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Zabha extension addresses these limitations by adding support for byte and halfword atomic memory operations to the RISC-V Unprivileged ISA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Zabha extension depends upon the Zaamo standard extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Zabha extension provides the `AMO[ADD|AND|OR|XOR|SWAP|MIN[U]|MAX[U]].[B|H]` instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: If Zacas extension is also implemented, Zabha further provides the `AMOCAS.[B|H]` instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: [wavedrom, zabha-ext-wavedrom-reg,svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 7, name: 'opcode', attr:['AMO','AMO','AMO','AMO','AMO','AMO','AMO','AMO']}, {bits: 5, name: 'rd', attr:['dest','dest','dest','dest','dest','dest','dest','dest']}, {bits: 3, name: 'funct3', attr:['width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1','width=0/1']}, {bits: 5, name: 'rs1', attr:['addr','addr','addr','addr','addr','addr','addr','addr']}, {bits: 5, name: 'rs2', attr:['src','src','src','src','src','src','src','src']}, {bits: 1, name: 'rl'}, {bits: 1, name: 'aq', attr:['ordering','ordering','ordering','ordering','ordering','ordering','ordering','ordering']}, {bits: 5, name: 'funct5', attr:['AMOSWAP.B/H','AMOADD.B/H','AMOAND.B/H','AMOOR.B/H','AMOXOR.B/H','AMOMAX[U].B/H','AMOMIN[U].B/H','AMOCAS.B/H']}, ], config:{lanes: 1, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: Byte and halfword AMOs always sign-extend the value placed in `rd`, and ignore the stem:[XLEN-1:2^{(width + 3)}] bits of the original value in `rs2`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: The `AMOCAS.[B|H]` instructions similarly ignore the stem:[XLEN-1:2^{(width + 3)}] bits of the original value in `rd`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: Similar to the AMOs specified in the A extension, the Zabha extension mandates that the address contained in the `rs1` register must be naturally aligned to the size of the operand.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: The same exception options as specified in the A extension are applicable in cases where the address is not naturally aligned.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions

sentences.text: Similar to the AMOs specified in the A and Zacas extensions, the AMOs in the Zabha extension optionally provide release consistency semantics, using the `aq` and `rl` bits, to help implement multiprocessor synchronization.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zabha" Extension for Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations, Version 1.0 > Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operation Instructions
